packages.name: puppeteer
packages.synopsis: Package puppeteer is a pure-Go, standard-library-only page-automation toolkit inspired by the Node.js Puppeteer API.
packages.doc:
Package puppeteer is a pure-Go, standard-library-only page-automation toolkit
inspired by the Node.js Puppeteer API. It fetches pages over net/http, parses
the returned HTML with its own tokenizer and DOM builder, and lets you query
and traverse the resulting document with a real CSS selector engine.

# No JavaScript, no rendering

This package deliberately does NOT run a browser and does NOT execute
JavaScript. A dependency-free Go library cannot embed a JavaScript engine or
a layout/rendering engine, so there is:

  - no script execution: content injected or mutated by client-side JS is
    never present in the DOM you query;
  - no rendering, layout, or painting: there are no element geometries,
    computed styles, screenshots, or visibility calculations;
  - no live DOM: the tree is a static snapshot of the bytes the server sent.

In practical terms, puppeteer here behaves like an HTTP client married to an
HTML parser and selector engine. It is ideal for scraping and automating
server-rendered pages, following links, submitting forms, and managing
cookies and headers. It is NOT a substitute for a headless Chromium when a
site's content depends on JavaScript. The WaitForSelector helper polls by
re-fetching the URL rather than observing a live DOM (see its documentation).

# Model

Launch returns a [Browser], which owns an *http.Client, a cookie jar
(net/http/cookiejar), shared headers, a user agent and a per-navigation
timeout. Browser.NewPage returns a [Page]. Page.Goto fetches a URL (following
redirects, updating cookies), reads the body and parses it into a DOM tree.

From a Page you can:

  - select nodes with QuerySelector / QuerySelectorAll;
  - read Content (raw HTML), HTML (re-serialized DOM), Title and URL;
  - enumerate resolved Links;
  - discover and fill Forms, then build or submit the resulting request.

Selected nodes are returned as [Element] handles offering TextContent,
InnerHTML, OuterHTML, Attr/Attributes, class helpers and DOM traversal
(Children, Parent, Next, Prev, Closest, Matches) plus node-relative
QuerySelector/QuerySelectorAll.

# Supported CSS selectors

The selector engine ([compileSelector] and the QuerySelector methods)
implements:

  - type selectors (div), the universal selector (*);
  - #id and .class;
  - attribute selectors [attr], [attr=val], [attr^=val], [attr$=val],
    [attr*=val], [attr~=val], [attr|=val];
  - combinators: descendant (space), child (>), adjacent sibling (+) and
    general sibling (~);
  - selector lists (grouping with commas);
  - structural pseudo-classes :first-child, :last-child and :nth-child(),
    including the An+B microsyntax (odd, even, 2n+1, -n+3, ...).

# HTML parsing

The tokenizer and parser are original code (they do not use golang.org/x/net).
They handle tags, quoted/unquoted attributes, comments, doctypes, void
elements, raw-text elements (script, style), common character references and
the most frequent implicit end-tag rules (for example <li> closing a previous
<li>). Parse never fails; malformed input is recovered the way browsers do.

# Example

A minimal end-to-end use looks like this:

	browser, _ := puppeteer.Launch(nil)
	page := browser.NewPage()
	if _, err := page.Goto("https://example.com"); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	fmt.Println(page.Title())
	links := page.Links()
	_ = links

See the package examples for a runnable version backed by httptest.

consts.doc: DefaultUserAgent is sent when LaunchOptions does not override it.

types.name: Attribute
types.doc:
Attribute is a single name/value pair on an element. Attribute names are
normalized to lower case during parsing.

types.name: Browser
types.doc:
Browser is a lightweight HTTP client with a cookie jar, shared headers and a
user agent. It is the entry point of the package and models Puppeteer's
Browser, minus any real rendering engine.

funcs.name: Launch
funcs.doc: Launch creates a Browser. Passing nil uses all defaults.

methods.name: Close
methods.doc: Close releases idle connections. The browser must not be used afterwards.

methods.name: Cookies
methods.doc: Cookies returns the cookies the jar would send for rawurl.

methods.name: NewPage
methods.doc: NewPage returns a fresh Page bound to this browser.

methods.name: SetCookies
methods.doc: SetCookies stores cookies in the jar for rawurl.

methods.name: SetExtraHTTPHeaders
methods.doc: SetExtraHTTPHeaders replaces the browser-level extra headers.

methods.name: SetUserAgent
methods.doc: SetUserAgent changes the user-agent sent on subsequent navigations.

methods.name: UserAgent
methods.doc: UserAgent returns the browser's configured user-agent string.

types.name: Element
types.doc:
Element is a handle to an ElementNode in a parsed document. It offers the
convenience accessors and traversal helpers familiar from Puppeteer's
ElementHandle, adapted to a static (non-JavaScript) DOM.

methods.name: Ancestors
methods.doc:
Ancestors returns the element's ancestor elements from the immediate parent up
to the document root's outermost element, nearest first. The document node
itself is not included.

methods.name: Attr
methods.doc: Attr returns the value of the named attribute and whether it exists.

methods.name: AttrOr
methods.doc: AttrOr returns the named attribute's value or def when it is absent.

methods.name: AttributeNames
methods.doc: AttributeNames returns the names of the element's attributes in source order.

methods.name: Attributes
methods.doc: Attributes returns all attributes as a name/value map.

methods.name: Children
methods.doc: Children returns the element's child elements (text and comments excluded).

methods.name: ClassList
methods.doc: ClassList returns the element's class tokens.

methods.name: Closest
methods.doc:
Closest returns the nearest ancestor (including the element itself) that
matches the selector, or nil.

methods.name: Dataset
methods.doc:
Dataset returns the element's data-* attributes as a map keyed by the DOM
dataset name: the "data-" prefix is stripped and each "-x" sequence becomes an
upper-cased letter, so data-user-id becomes userId. This mirrors the
HTMLElement.dataset property. Attributes without the data- prefix are omitted.

methods.name: HasAttribute
methods.doc:
HasAttribute reports whether the element carries the named attribute,
regardless of its value. Names are matched case-insensitively.

methods.name: HasClass
methods.doc: HasClass reports whether the element carries the given class token.

methods.name: ID
methods.doc: ID returns the element's id attribute.

methods.name: InnerHTML
methods.doc: InnerHTML returns the serialized markup of the element's children.

methods.name: InnerText
methods.doc:
InnerText is a convenience alias for TextContent with surrounding whitespace
trimmed and internal runs collapsed to single spaces.

methods.name: IsEmpty
methods.doc:
IsEmpty reports whether the element has no child elements, no comments and no
non-whitespace text, approximating the CSS :empty pseudo-class with the
Selectors Level 4 relaxation that whitespace-only text is treated as empty.

methods.name: Matches
methods.doc: Matches reports whether the element matches the selector.

methods.name: Next
methods.doc: Next returns the next sibling element, or nil.

methods.name: NextAll
methods.doc: NextAll returns every following sibling element in document order.

methods.name: Node
methods.doc: Node returns the underlying DOM node.

methods.name: OuterHTML
methods.doc: OuterHTML returns the serialized markup of the element including itself.

methods.name: Parent
methods.doc: Parent returns the element's parent element, or nil.

methods.name: Prev
methods.doc: Prev returns the previous sibling element, or nil.

methods.name: PrevAll
methods.doc:
PrevAll returns every preceding sibling element in document order (the
nearest preceding sibling last).

methods.name: QuerySelector
methods.doc: QuerySelector returns the first descendant matching the selector, or nil.

methods.name: QuerySelectorAll
methods.doc: QuerySelectorAll returns all descendants matching the selector.

methods.name: Siblings
methods.doc:
Siblings returns the element's sibling elements in document order, excluding
the element itself. Text and comment nodes are skipped.

methods.name: TagName
methods.doc: TagName returns the element's lower-cased tag name.

methods.name: TextContent
methods.doc: TextContent returns the concatenated text of the element and its descendants.

types.name: Form
types.doc:
Form is a discovered <form> together with its resolved action, method and the
current values of its fields. It can build (and optionally send) the request
that submitting it would produce.

methods.name: BuildRequest
methods.doc:
BuildRequest constructs the *http.Request that submitting the form produces.
For GET the values are encoded into the query string; for POST they form a
urlencoded body with the appropriate Content-Type. The request is not sent.

methods.name: FieldNames
methods.doc: FieldNames returns the names of all controls in document order.

methods.name: Get
methods.doc: Get returns the current value of the named field and whether it exists.

methods.name: Set
methods.doc:
Set assigns a value to the named field, marking it for inclusion. A new field
is created if the name is unknown, which allows adding parameters the static
markup did not declare.

methods.name: Submit
methods.doc:
Submit builds the form request, sends it through the browser and loads the
response into the originating page.

methods.name: SubmitContext
methods.doc: SubmitContext is Submit with an explicit context.

methods.name: Values
methods.doc: Values returns the url.Values that this form would submit.

types.name: LaunchOptions
types.doc:
LaunchOptions configures a Browser. The zero value is valid and yields a
browser with sensible defaults.

types.name: Node
types.signature:
type Node struct {
	Type     NodeType
	Data     string
	Attr     []Attribute
	Parent   *Node
	Children []*Node
}
types.doc:
Node is a single node in the DOM tree produced by the parser. The tree is a
simple parent/children model: every Node (except the document root) has a
Parent, and Children preserves document order.

For an ElementNode, Data holds the (lower-cased) tag name. For a TextNode,
Data holds the raw text. For a CommentNode, Data holds the comment body
(without the <!-- --> delimiters). For a DoctypeNode, Data holds the doctype
name.

funcs.name: Parse
funcs.doc:
Parse turns an HTML document string into a DOM tree rooted at a DocumentNode.
It never returns an error: malformed input is recovered pragmatically (the
way browsers do) rather than rejected.

methods.name: AppendChild
methods.doc: AppendChild adds c as the last child of n and sets c.Parent.

types.name: NodeType
types.doc: NodeType identifies the kind of a DOM Node.

types.name: Page
types.doc:
Page is a single navigable document. It holds the most recently fetched HTML,
its parsed DOM tree and the resolved URL, and exposes selection, link and
form helpers over that document.

methods.name: CanGoBack
methods.doc:
CanGoBack reports whether there is an earlier entry in the session history to
navigate back to.

methods.name: CanGoForward
methods.doc:
CanGoForward reports whether there is a later entry in the session history to
navigate forward to.

methods.name: Content
methods.doc: Content returns the raw HTML body of the current page.

methods.name: Cookies
methods.doc:
Cookies returns the cookies the browser's jar would send for the page's
current URL, mirroring Puppeteer's page.cookies. It returns an error when no
page has been loaded yet.

methods.name: Count
methods.doc:
Count returns the number of elements matching selector in the current
document. It reports an error only when the selector is invalid or no
document is loaded.

methods.name: Document
methods.doc: Document returns the root DocumentNode of the parsed page, or nil.

methods.name: FillForm
methods.signature: func (p *Page) FillForm(selector string, values map[string]string) (*Form, error)
methods.doc:
FillForm looks up a form by selector, applies values and returns it ready to
build or submit. It mirrors Puppeteer's ergonomics of filling a form in one
call.

methods.name: FormBySelector
methods.doc:
FormBySelector returns the first form matching selector, or an error if none
match.

methods.name: Forms
methods.doc: Forms discovers every <form> on the page.

methods.name: GetAttribute
methods.signature: func (p *Page) GetAttribute(selector, name string) (string, bool, error)
methods.doc:
GetAttribute returns the value of attribute name on the first element matching
selector, mirroring Playwright's page.getAttribute. The boolean is false when
no element matches or the element lacks the attribute; an error is returned
only for an invalid selector or a missing document.

methods.name: GoBack
methods.doc:
GoBack navigates to the previous entry in the session history, re-fetching it
like Puppeteer's page.goBack. It returns (nil, nil) when there is no earlier
entry, matching Puppeteer's convention of resolving to null.

methods.name: GoBackContext
methods.doc: GoBackContext is GoBack with an explicit context for cancellation.

methods.name: GoForward
methods.doc:
GoForward navigates to the next entry in the session history, re-fetching it
like Puppeteer's page.goForward. It returns (nil, nil) when there is no later
entry.

methods.name: GoForwardContext
methods.doc: GoForwardContext is GoForward with an explicit context for cancellation.

methods.name: Goto
methods.doc:
Goto navigates the page to rawurl using GET and parses the response body as
HTML. Relative URLs are resolved against the page's current URL when one
exists.

methods.name: GotoContext
methods.signature: func (p *Page) GotoContext(ctx context.Context, rawurl string) (*Response, error)
methods.doc: GotoContext is Goto with an explicit context for cancellation.

methods.name: HTML
methods.doc: HTML returns the serialized DOM of the current page.

methods.name: History
methods.doc:
History returns a copy of the URLs in the session history, oldest first. The
current entry is History()[index] where index can be derived from the length
and the availability reported by CanGoBack/CanGoForward.

methods.name: Images
methods.doc: Images returns every source on the page resolved to an absolute URL, de-duplicated while preserving first-seen order.

methods.name: Links
methods.doc:
Links returns every href on the page resolved to an absolute URL. Duplicates
are removed while preserving first-seen order.

methods.name: MetaContent
methods.doc:
MetaContent returns the content of the first <meta> tag whose name or property
attribute equals key (case-insensitive), and whether such a tag was found.

methods.name: Metas
methods.doc:
Metas returns the document's <meta> metadata as a name-to-content map. Both
the name attribute and the Open Graph style property attribute act as keys
(property takes precedence when both are present on one tag); tags without a
content attribute or without either key are skipped. Later duplicates win.

methods.name: NormalizedHTML
methods.doc:
NormalizedHTML serializes the page's document the way a browser would report document.documentElement.outerHTML — i.e. what Puppeteer's page.content() returns. Any leading is preserved, a single element is guaranteed, and missing / elements are synthesized so that, for example, a document set from the fragment "
hello
" serializes as "
hello
". Unlike HTML, which serializes the parse tree verbatim, NormalizedHTML applies the head/body structural normalization that a live DOM performs. It does not mutate the underlying document. When the page has no document it returns "".

methods.name: QuerySelector
methods.doc: QuerySelector returns the first element matching selector, or nil.

methods.name: QuerySelectorAll
methods.doc: QuerySelectorAll returns all elements matching selector in document order.

methods.name: Reload
methods.doc:
Reload re-fetches the current URL and re-parses the document, mirroring
Puppeteer's page.reload. The history cursor is left unchanged: a reload does
not create a new history entry. It returns an error if no page has been
loaded yet.

methods.name: ReloadContext
methods.doc: ReloadContext is Reload with an explicit context for cancellation.

methods.name: Scripts
methods.doc:
Scripts returns every external <script src> on the page resolved to an
absolute URL, de-duplicated while preserving first-seen order. Inline scripts
(which have no src) are omitted.

methods.name: SetExtraHTTPHeaders
methods.doc: SetExtraHTTPHeaders sets page-level headers that augment the browser's.

methods.name: SetUserAgent
methods.doc: SetUserAgent overrides the user agent for this page only.

methods.name: Stylesheets
methods.doc:
Stylesheets returns the href of every <link rel="stylesheet"> resolved to an
absolute URL, de-duplicated while preserving first-seen order.

methods.name: TextContent
methods.doc:
TextContent returns the concatenated text of the first element matching
selector, mirroring Playwright's page.textContent. The boolean is false when
no element matches; an error is returned only for an invalid selector or a
missing document.

methods.name: Title
methods.doc: Title returns the trimmed text of the document's <title>, if any.

methods.name: URL
methods.doc: URL returns the page's current absolute URL, or "" before any navigation.

methods.name: WaitForSelector
methods.doc:
WaitForSelector re-fetches the current URL on an interval until selector
matches or the timeout elapses. Because this package executes no JavaScript,
a page's DOM cannot change on its own; this helper is therefore only useful
for content that varies across identical requests (polling an endpoint). It
returns the first matching element.

types.name: Response
types.signature:
type Response struct {
	URL        *url.URL
	Status     string
	StatusCode int
	Header     http.Header
	Body       []byte
}
types.doc: Response captures the result of a navigation.

methods.name: OK
methods.doc: OK reports whether the response status is in the 2xx range.

types.name: WaitForSelectorOptions

examples.code:
package main import ( "fmt" "io" "log" "net/http" "net/http/httptest" "github.com/malcolmston/puppeteer" ) func main() { srv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, _ *http.Request) { _, _ = io.WriteString(w, `Demo

Fruits

  • Apple
  • Banana
  • Cherry
`) })) defer srv.Close() browser, err := puppeteer.Launch(nil) if err != nil { log.Fatal(err) } defer func() { _ = browser.Close() }() page := browser.NewPage() if _, err := page.Goto(srv.URL); err != nil { log.Fatal(err) } fmt.Println("title:", page.Title()) h1, _ := page.QuerySelector("h1.title") fmt.Println("heading:", h1.TextContent()) fruits, _ := page.QuerySelectorAll("li.fruit") for _, f := range fruits { fmt.Println("fruit:", f.TextContent()) } selected, _ := page.QuerySelector("li:nth-child(2)") fmt.Println("selected:", selected.TextContent()) }
examples.output:
title: Demo
heading: Fruits
fruit: Apple
fruit: Banana
fruit: Cherry
selected: Banana
examples.doc:
Example demonstrates launching a browser, navigating to a page served by
httptest, and selecting nodes with the CSS selector engine.
